# Glyphic — Full Documentation

Generated: 2026-05-02T17:12:02.839Z

---

## /docs/_planned/config

---
title: "@config (planned)"
description: Global diagram configuration — font, colors, animation defaults. Not yet implemented.
---

import { TypeTable } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/type-table'

# @config

<Callout type="warn">
**Not yet implemented.** The syntax below is reserved — do not ship diagrams relying on it. This page exists to document planned behaviour before implementation.
</Callout>

The `@config` block sets global diagram defaults: font, primary colors, animation defaults, and Mermaid base theme. Per-edge and per-node `@animate` entries always override global defaults.

## Font

```text
@config
font: IBM Plex Sans
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    font: {
      description: "Diagram label font — any Google Fonts family name",
      type: "string",
      default: "IBM Plex Sans",
    },
  }}
/>

## Colors

```text
@config
primaryColor: #3b82f6
lineColor: #6b7280
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    primaryColor: {
      description: "Node fill / primary color",
      type: "CSS color",
      default: "theme default",
    },
    lineColor: {
      description: "Edge stroke color",
      type: "CSS color",
      default: "theme default",
    },
  }}
/>

## Mermaid base theme

```text
@config
theme: dark
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    theme: {
      description: "Mermaid base theme",
      type: '"dark" | "default" | "forest" | "neutral" | "base"',
      default: "dark",
    },
  }}
/>

## Global animation defaults

Sets baseline defaults for all animated edges. Per-edge `@animate` entries override these for individual edges.

```text
@config
animation.dot.color: #f59e0b
animation.dot.speed: 1.2
animation.lineFlow.color: #3b82f6
animation.lineFlow.speed: 2
animation.lineFlow.motionPreset: balanced
animation.lineFlow.glowPreset: none
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    "animation.dot.color": { description: "Default dot color", type: "CSS color", default: "#3b82f6" },
    "animation.dot.speed": { description: "Default dot traversal duration in seconds", type: "number", default: "1.5" },
    "animation.lineFlow.color": { description: "Default segment color", type: "CSS color", default: "#3b82f6" },
    "animation.lineFlow.speed": { description: "Default cycle duration in seconds", type: "number", default: "1.5" },
    "animation.lineFlow.motionPreset": { description: "Default motion character", type: '"flash" | "balanced" | "long-hold"', default: "balanced" },
    "animation.lineFlow.glowPreset": { description: "Default glow", type: '"none" | "subtle" | "strong"', default: "none" },
  }}
/>


---

## /docs/_planned/simulate

---
title: "@simulate (planned)"
description: Traffic pattern simulation with dynamic edge speed and node stress indicators. Not yet implemented.
---

# @simulate

<Callout type="warn">
**Not yet implemented.** The syntax below is reserved — do not ship diagrams relying on it. This page exists to document planned behaviour before implementation.
</Callout>

The `@simulate` block runs a traffic simulation on the diagram — dynamically varying edge animation speed and node stress state based on configurable load patterns.

Full syntax and parameter reference will be added when the feature ships.


---

## /docs/getting-started/editor-basics

---
title: Editor Basics
description: The editor layout, toolbar, keyboard shortcuts, file upload, and the Examples dropdown — everything you need to work efficiently in Glyphic.
---

import { Accordions, Accordion } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/accordion'

## Layout

The editor is a two-column layout: source code on the left, live preview on the right. The toolbar runs across the top.

```
[Logo] [Examples ▼]          [Share] [Export ▼] [Light/Dark] [Grid] [Prettify]
┌─────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────┐
│                     │                             │
│   Source editor     │     Live preview            │
│   (code panel)      │     (diagram panel)         │
│                     │                             │
└─────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────┘
```

The preview updates as you type — no save or render button needed.

## Toolbar

<Accordions>
  <Accordion title="Examples — load a pre-built diagram">

Click **Examples** to open a dropdown of curated diagrams. Selecting one loads the full source (including any meta-blocks) into the editor.

Use examples to learn meta-block syntax or to start from a working template.

  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Share — create a shareable link">

Click **Share** to generate a link to your diagram.

- **Editable** — anyone with the link can view and edit the diagram
- **View Only** — the diagram renders but the source is never exposed to the viewer

See [Sharing](/docs/guides/sharing) for full details and embed snippets.

  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Export — download your diagram">

Click **Export** to download in your chosen format. Animated exports (GIF, WebM) capture the running animation from the preview.

See [Exporting](/docs/guides/exporting) for format details.

  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Light/Dark — toggle editor theme">

Switches between light and dark themes for both the editor panel and the preview background. The Mermaid theme updates to match.

  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Grid — toggle dot grid background">

Shows or hides the dot grid on the preview background. The grid is not included in exports.

  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Prettify — render with R DiagrammeR">

Renders the diagram using R DiagrammeR instead of the default Mermaid renderer. Produces a different visual style. Not all diagram types are supported.

  </Accordion>
</Accordions>

## Keyboard shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `⌘Z` / `Ctrl+Z` | Undo |
| `⌘⇧Z` / `Ctrl⇧Z` | Redo |
| `⌘+` / `Ctrl+` | Zoom in |
| `⌘-` / `Ctrl-` | Zoom out |
| `⌘0` / `Ctrl0` | Reset zoom |

## File upload

Drag a `.mmd` or `.txt` file onto the editor panel to load it. You can also click the upload area to browse for a file. The file contents replace the current source.

## Zoom controls

Use the floating zoom controls in the bottom-right of the preview panel to zoom and pan. Click the reset button to fit the diagram to the panel.


---

## /docs/getting-started

---
title: Getting Started
description: Everything you need to start using Glyphic — from your first diagram to animated exports.
---

import { Card, Cards } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/card'

<Cards>
  <Card title="Quickstart" href="/docs/getting-started/quickstart" description="Render your first animated diagram in under two minutes." />
  <Card title="Editor Basics" href="/docs/getting-started/editor-basics" description="Toolbar, keyboard shortcuts, file upload, zoom controls." />
</Cards>


---

## /docs/getting-started/quickstart

---
title: Quickstart
description: Render your first Mermaid diagram — with animation — in under two minutes.
---

import { Steps, Step } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/steps'

<Steps>
  <Step>

## Open the editor

Go to [glyphic.cc/app](https://glyphic.cc/app). The editor opens with an example diagram pre-loaded in the left panel and a live preview on the right.

  </Step>
  <Step>

## Write a diagram

Clear the editor and paste:

```text
flowchart LR
  User[User] --> LB[Load Balancer]
  LB --> S1[Server 1]
  LB --> S2[Server 2]
  S1 --> DB[(Database)]
  S2 --> DB
```

The preview updates instantly.

  </Step>
  <Step>

## Add an animation

Append this block below your diagram (after the last line, no blank line required):

```text
@animate
User-->LB: type=line-flow, color=#38bdf8, speed=2
LB-->S1: type=line-flow, color=#22c55e, speed=2.5
LB-->S2: type=line-flow, color=#22c55e, speed=2.5
```

The edges begin animating in the preview immediately.

  </Step>
  <Step>

## Export

Click **Export** in the toolbar and choose a format:

| Format | Best for |
|--------|----------|
| SVG | Docs, READMEs, embedding |
| PNG | Slide decks, PR comments |
| GIF | Animated, universal support |
| WebM | High-quality video, Notion embeds |

  </Step>
</Steps>

## Next steps

- [Editor Basics](/docs/getting-started/editor-basics) — toolbar, keyboard shortcuts, file upload
- [@animate reference](/docs/meta-blocks/animate) — full parameter reference for edge and node animations


---

## /docs/guides/exporting

---
title: Exporting
description: Export your diagram as SVG, PNG, GIF, or WebM. Each format has a different use case.
---

import { Accordions, Accordion } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/accordion'

Click **Export** in the toolbar to download your diagram. Animated exports capture the running animation from the preview.

## Formats

<Accordions>
  <Accordion title="SVG — scalable, embeddable">

SVG exports the diagram as a scalable vector graphic. The file is self-contained and can be embedded in HTML, Markdown (GitHub, Notion), or any docs platform.

**Best for:** Documentation, READMEs, web pages.

**Note:** SVG exports are static — animations and meta-block effects are not included.

  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="PNG — raster image">

PNG exports the diagram as a fixed-resolution raster image.

**Best for:** Slide decks, PR comments, screenshots.

**Note:** PNG exports are static.

  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="GIF — animated, universal">

GIF captures the running animation from the preview panel and exports it as a looping animated GIF.

**Best for:** Sharing animations in contexts that don't support video (email, Jira, GitHub issues).

  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="WebM — high-quality animated video">

WebM captures the running animation as a high-quality video file.

**Best for:** Notion embeds, Loom uploads, presentations where video quality matters.

  </Accordion>
</Accordions>


---

## /docs/guides

---
title: Guides
description: Step-by-step walkthroughs for sharing and exporting your Glyphic diagrams.
---

| Guide | What you'll learn |
|---|---|
| [Sharing](/docs/guides/sharing) | Editable links, View Only links, embed snippets |
| [Exporting](/docs/guides/exporting) | SVG, PNG, GIF, WebM — formats and when to use each |


---

## /docs/guides/sharing

---
title: Sharing
description: Share your diagram as an editable link or a view-only embed. Learn the difference and how to generate each.
---

import { Steps, Step } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/steps'
import { Tabs, Tab } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/tabs'

Glyphic has two share modes:

| Mode | Who can edit | Source visible to viewer |
|------|-------------|--------------------------|
| Editable | Anyone with the link | Yes |
| View Only | No one (read-only) | No — rendered server-side |

## Creating a share link

<Steps>
  <Step>

## Open the Share modal

Click **Share** in the toolbar.

  </Step>
  <Step>

## Choose a mode

Select **Editable** or **View Only**.

  </Step>
  <Step>

## Copy the link

Copy the generated URL. Anyone with the link can open the diagram.

  </Step>
</Steps>

## Embedding

<Tabs items={["Script tag", "iframe"]}>
  <Tab value="Script tag">

Use the `embed.js` loader for Notion, Confluence, or any page that allows `<script>` tags:

```html
<script src="https://glyphic.cc/embed.js"
  data-src="YOUR_SHARE_ID"
  data-theme="dark">
</script>
```

The script reads its own `data-*` attributes and replaces itself with an `<iframe>`.

| Attribute | Default | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `data-src` | required | Share ID from the share URL |
| `data-theme` | `dark` | Theme hint |
| `data-width` | `800` | iframe width (px or %) |
| `data-height` | `500` | iframe height (px) |

  </Tab>
  <Tab value="iframe">

For full control, use the `<iframe>` directly:

```html
<iframe
  src="https://glyphic.cc/embed/YOUR_SHARE_ID"
  width="800"
  height="500"
  frameborder="0">
</iframe>
```

  </Tab>
</Tabs>

<Callout type="info">
The embed URL (`/embed/YOUR_SHARE_ID`) renders a chromeless version of the shared diagram — no header, no toolbar, no zoom controls. All meta-blocks (`@animate`, `@present`, `@compare`) work identically to the full editor view.
</Callout>

<Callout type="warn">
GitHub READMEs block iframes. A static `preview.svg` badge endpoint is planned.
</Callout>


---

## /docs

---
title: Glyphic Documentation
description: Glyphic is a visual editor for Mermaid diagrams. Build, animate, and share architecture diagrams without leaving your browser.
---

import { Card, Cards } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/card'

# Glyphic Documentation

Glyphic turns plain-text Mermaid diagrams into living, interactive visuals — with animation, status overlays, presentation labels, and before/after comparison built in.

## Start here

<Cards>
  <Card title="Quickstart" href="/docs/getting-started/quickstart" description="Render your first animated diagram in under two minutes." />
  <Card title="Editor Basics" href="/docs/getting-started/editor-basics" description="Learn the toolbar, keyboard shortcuts, and file upload." />
</Cards>

## Meta-blocks

Glyphic extends Mermaid with meta-blocks — directives appended after the diagram definition.

<Cards>
  <Card title="@animate" href="/docs/meta-blocks/animate" description="Animate edges with flowing particles and nodes with pulsing borders." />
  <Card title="@status" href="/docs/meta-blocks/status" description="Live NOC board — map nodes to healthy, degraded, or down states." />
  <Card title="@compare" href="/docs/meta-blocks/compare" description="Before/after split view with optional diff highlighting." />
  <Card title="@present" href="/docs/meta-blocks/present" description="Add badges and fixed overlays to your diagram." />
  <Card title="@style" href="/docs/meta-blocks/style" description="Set static node outline colors." />
</Cards>

## Guides

<Cards>
  <Card title="Sharing" href="/docs/guides/sharing" description="Editable links, View Only links, and embed snippets." />
  <Card title="Exporting" href="/docs/guides/exporting" description="SVG, PNG, GIF, and WebM — formats and when to use each." />
</Cards>


---

## /docs/meta-blocks/animate

---
title: "@animate"
description: Animate edges and nodes in your Mermaid diagram with flowing particles, pulsing borders, and status indicator dots.
---

import { Tabs, Tab } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/tabs'
import { Accordions, Accordion } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/accordion'
import { TypeTable } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/type-table'

The `@animate` block controls animations on individual edges and nodes. Each line targets either an edge (`A-->B`) or a node (`NodeId`).

```text
@animate
Client-->Server: type=line-flow, color=#38bdf8, speed=2
Server: type=node-pulse, pulseStyle=ripple, color=#ef4444, speed=1.4
```

<Tabs items={["Edge animations", "Node animations"]}>
  <Tab value="Edge animations">

## Edge animations

```text
@animate
{from}-->{to}: type={type}, [param=value, ...]
```

<Accordions>
  <Accordion title="type=line-flow — segment draws on, holds, then retracts">

A segment of the edge animates: draws on, holds, then retracts and fades. Repeats indefinitely.

```text
@animate
Client-->Server: type=line-flow, color=#38bdf8, speed=2, preset=balanced, glow=subtle
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    color: {
      description: "Line segment color",
      type: "CSS color",
      default: "#3b82f6",
    },
    speed: {
      description: "Duration of one full cycle in seconds",
      type: "number",
      default: "1.5",
    },
    preset: {
      description: "Motion character",
      type: '"flash" | "balanced" | "long-hold"',
      default: "balanced",
    },
    glow: {
      description: "Blur filter around the segment",
      type: '"none" | "subtle" | "strong"',
      default: "none",
    },
  }}
/>

**Presets:**

| Preset | Character |
|--------|-----------|
| `flash` | Short segment, fast travel — urgent, rapid traffic |
| `balanced` | Medium segment, moderate speed — default working state |
| `long-hold` | Long segment, slow travel — steady, high-volume throughput |

  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="type=dot — small dot travels along the edge">

```text
@animate
A-->B: type=dot, color=#f59e0b, speed=1.2
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    color: {
      description: "Dot fill color",
      type: "CSS color",
      default: "#3b82f6",
    },
    speed: {
      description: "Duration per traversal in seconds",
      type: "number",
      default: "1.5",
    },
  }}
/>

  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="type=pill — rounded rectangle travels tangent-aligned">

A rounded rectangle travels along the edge, rotating to stay tangent-aligned to the path direction. Supports an optional halo glow.

```text
@animate
A-->B: type=pill, color=#4fc3f7, speed=1.5
B-->C: type=pill, color=#7ee787, speed=1, glow=strong
C-->D: type=pill, color=#f59e0b, speed=0.8, glow=subtle, width=24, height=12
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    color: {
      description: "Pill fill color",
      type: "CSS color",
      default: "#4fc3f7",
    },
    speed: {
      description: "Duration per full traversal in seconds",
      type: "number",
      default: "2",
    },
    glow: {
      description: "Halo blur around the pill",
      type: '"none" | "subtle" | "strong"',
      default: "subtle",
    },
    width: {
      description: "Pill length along the travel axis in px",
      type: "number",
      default: "28",
    },
    height: {
      description: "Pill thickness in px; corner radius = height / 2",
      type: "number",
      default: "14",
    },
  }}
/>

<Callout type="info">
Pills use the CSS Motion Path spec (`offset-path` + `offset-rotate: auto`) for tangent-aligned travel. They follow curves and diagonal edges and rotate to face the direction of travel at every point.
</Callout>

  </Accordion>
</Accordions>

  </Tab>
  <Tab value="Node animations">

## Node animations

Node animations target a node by its ID (not an edge).

<Accordions>
  <Accordion title="type=node-pulse — pulsing border, fill, shadow, flash, or ripple">

```text
@animate
{nodeId}: type=node-pulse, [param=value, ...]
```

```text
@animate
Server: type=node-pulse, pulseStyle=border, color=#ef4444, speed=1.5
Server: type=node-pulse, pulseStyle=flash,  color=#ef4444, speed=1
Server: type=node-pulse, pulseStyle=ripple, color=#ef4444, speed=1.4, rippleCount=3
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    pulseStyle: {
      description: "Visual effect",
      type: '"border" | "fill" | "shadow" | "flash" | "ripple"',
      default: "border",
    },
    color: {
      description: "Pulse or ring color",
      type: "CSS color",
      default: "#3b82f6",
    },
    speed: {
      description: "Duration of one cycle in seconds",
      type: "number",
      default: "1.5",
    },
    rippleCount: {
      description: "Number of staggered rings (ripple style only)",
      type: "1 | 2 | 3",
      default: "1",
    },
  }}
/>

**`pulseStyle` variants:**

| Style | Effect | Use case |
|-------|--------|----------|
| `border` | Smooth opacity oscillation on a border ring | Persistent warning, attention state |
| `fill` | Animates the fill opacity of the node shape | Highlighting, selection |
| `shadow` | Border ring with Gaussian blur glow | Emphasis, "active" state |
| `flash` | Hard on/off strobe | Urgent alert, error state |
| `ripple` | Expanding rings radiate outward and fade | Broadcasting, active transmission |

<Callout type="info">
**`flash` color tip:** Choose a color that contrasts with the node's static border. If the flash color matches the existing `classDef` stroke or `@style outline` color, the strobe will be invisible.
</Callout>

  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="type=oval-inset — filled oval in the left cap of a stadium node">

A filled oval injected into the left cap of a stadium (pill-shaped) node. Use with nodes declared using the `([text])` stadium syntax.

```text
@animate
S1: type=oval-inset, color=#22c55e
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    color: {
      description: "Fill color of the oval",
      type: "CSS color",
      default: "#3b82f6",
    },
  }}
/>

<Callout type="warn">
The node must use stadium shape (`([NodeId])`) syntax in the flowchart. On standard rect-shaped nodes the oval will appear misaligned.
</Callout>

Combine with `classDef` for full control over border and fill:

```text
flowchart LR
  S1([S1])
  classDef serverNode fill:#052e16,stroke:#22c55e,stroke-width:2px,color:#e2e8f0
  class S1 serverNode

@animate
S1: type=oval-inset, color=#22c55e
```

  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="type=status-dot — animated indicator dot on any node">

A small animated dot injected into or adjacent to any node shape. Supports four animation styles and seven positions.

```text
@animate
GW: type=status-dot, style=breathe, color=#22c55e, position=left, size=10
Auth: type=status-dot, style=sonar, color=#f59e0b, position=left, rippleCount=2, label="Auth Service"
Payment: type=status-dot, style=blink, color=#ef4444, position=left, label="Payment — down"
legend dot: position=below-center, padding=16
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    style: {
      description: "Animation style",
      type: '"breathe" | "blink" | "sonar" | "ripple"',
      default: "breathe",
    },
    color: {
      description: "Dot fill and ring color",
      type: "CSS color",
      default: "#3b82f6",
    },
    position: {
      description: "Placement relative to the node",
      type: '"left" | "right" | "top-left" | "top-right" | "top-center" | "left-inset" | "center"',
      default: "left",
    },
    size: {
      description: "Dot diameter in px",
      type: "number",
      default: "10",
    },
    speed: {
      description: "One animation cycle duration in seconds",
      type: "number",
      default: "2",
    },
    rippleCount: {
      description: "Number of staggered rings (sonar and ripple styles only)",
      type: "1 | 2 | 3",
      default: "1",
    },
    label: {
      description: "Legend row text — omit to exclude from legend",
      type: "string",
      default: "—",
    },
  }}
/>

**Animation styles:**

| Style | Effect | Use case |
|-------|--------|----------|
| `breathe` | Smooth opacity oscillation | Soft, persistent indicator |
| `blink` | Hard on/off strobe | Urgent alert, error |
| `sonar` | Expanding stroked rings | Broadcasting, active transmission |
| `ripple` | Expanding filled semi-transparent rings | Softer broadcast |

**`legend dot:` line**

When present, renders a floating chip row showing nodes that have a `label=` value. Nodes without `label=` are omitted. The legend is not included in SVG/PNG exports.

```text
legend dot: position=below-center, padding=16
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    position: {
      description: "Legend panel anchor",
      type: '"top-left" | "top-center" | "top-right" | "middle-left" | "middle-right" | "below-left" | "below-center" | "below-right"',
      default: "below-center",
    },
    padding: {
      description: "Offset from panel edge in px",
      type: "number",
      default: "12",
    },
  }}
/>

  </Accordion>
</Accordions>

  </Tab>
</Tabs>


---

## /docs/meta-blocks/compare

---
title: "@compare"
description: Show two diagram states side-by-side with before/after split view and optional diff highlighting.
---

import { TypeTable } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/type-table'

The `@compare split` marker splits a single `.mmd` file into two diagram states shown side-by-side. Everything above the marker is the "before" diagram; everything below (up to `@compare`) is the "after" diagram.

## Syntax

```text
{before diagram source}
{optional meta-blocks for before}

@compare split

{after diagram source}
{optional meta-blocks for after}

@compare
title before: "{label}"
title after: "{label}"
```

## Example

```text
flowchart LR
  A --> Server

@animate
A-->Server: type=line-flow, color=#ef4444, speed=1

@present
badge Server: "⚠ Overloaded" type=error

@compare split

flowchart LR
  A --> LB --> S1
  A --> LB --> S2

@animate
A-->LB: type=line-flow, color=#38bdf8, speed=2
LB-->S1: type=line-flow, color=#22c55e, speed=2.5
LB-->S2: type=line-flow, color=#22c55e, speed=2.5

@present
overlay top-right: "✓ Balanced" position=fixed color=#10b981

@compare
title before: "⚠ Congested"
title after: "✓ Load Balanced"
```

## `@compare` block parameters

<TypeTable
  type={{
    "title before": {
      description: "Label shown above the left (before) panel",
      type: "string",
      default: "—",
    },
    "title after": {
      description: "Label shown above the right (after) panel",
      type: "string",
      default: "—",
    },
    diff: {
      description: "Enable automatic diff highlighting between the two states",
      type: "true | false",
      default: "false",
    },
  }}
/>

## Diff highlighting

Add `diff: true` to the `@compare` block to automatically highlight structural changes:

- **Before panel** — nodes and edges absent from the after diagram are highlighted red
- **After panel** — nodes and edges new in the after diagram are highlighted green
- Shared nodes and edges render without any highlight

A legend (`● added  ● removed`) appears at the bottom of the split view when `diff: true` is set.

```text
@compare
title before: "⚠ Congested"
title after: "✓ Load Balanced"
diff: true
```


---

## /docs/meta-blocks

---
title: Meta-blocks
description: Glyphic extends Mermaid with meta-blocks — structured directives that add animation, status overlays, presentation labels, and split-view comparison without changing your Mermaid source.
---

import { Card, Cards } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/card'

Meta-blocks are Glyphic-specific extensions appended **after** the diagram definition. They use a simple keyword syntax:

```text
flowchart LR
  A --> B --> C

@animate
A-->B: type=line-flow, color=#38bdf8, speed=2

@present
badge B: "Processing" type=info

@compare split

flowchart LR
  A --> B

@compare
title before: "Before"
title after: "After"
```

<Callout type="info">
**Mermaid compatible.** Strip everything from the first `@` line downward and you have valid, portable Mermaid that renders in any standard renderer. Meta-blocks are purely additive.
</Callout>

## Directives

<Cards>
  <Card title="@animate" href="/docs/meta-blocks/animate" description="Animate edges with flowing particles and nodes with pulsing borders or status dots." />
  <Card title="@style" href="/docs/meta-blocks/style" description="Set static visual styles on nodes — outline color and more." />
  <Card title="@status" href="/docs/meta-blocks/status" description="Turn your diagram into a live NOC board by mapping nodes to health states." />
  <Card title="@present" href="/docs/meta-blocks/present" description="Add badges to nodes and fixed text overlays on the canvas." />
  <Card title="@compare" href="/docs/meta-blocks/compare" description="Show two diagram states side-by-side with optional diff highlighting." />
</Cards>

## Syntax rules

- Meta-blocks follow the diagram definition — never before it
- Each block opens with `@keyword` on its own line
- Entries continue until the next `@keyword` or end of file
- Meta-blocks are case-sensitive
- Multiple meta-blocks can appear in the same file


---

## /docs/meta-blocks/present

---
title: "@present"
description: Add presentation overlays to your diagram — badges attached to nodes and fixed text labels positioned on the canvas.
---

import { Tabs, Tab } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/tabs'
import { TypeTable } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/type-table'

The `@present` block adds visual labels to the diagram: badges attached to nodes and fixed overlays positioned on the canvas.

```text
@present
badge {nodeId}: "{text}" [type={type}] [color={color}] [position={position}]
overlay {anchor}: "{text}" [color={color}] position=fixed
```

<Tabs items={["badge", "overlay"]}>
  <Tab value="badge">

## `badge`

A pill-shaped label rendered in the SVG, attached to a node and moving with it.

```text
@present
badge Server: "⚠ Overloaded" type=error
badge LB: "Load Balancer" type=info
badge DB: "Healthy" type=success color=#10b981
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    type: {
      description: "Preset color scheme",
      type: '"info" | "success" | "warning" | "error"',
      default: "—",
    },
    color: {
      description: "Override badge background color",
      type: "CSS color",
      default: "grey",
    },
    position: {
      description: "Attached moves with diagram; fixed stays in viewport",
      type: '"attached" | "fixed"',
      default: "attached",
    },
  }}
/>

**Type color presets:**

| Type | Background | Text |
|------|-----------|------|
| `info` | `#3b82f6` (blue) | white |
| `success` | `#10b981` (green) | white |
| `warning` | `#f59e0b` (amber) | black |
| `error` | `#ef4444` (red) | white |

  </Tab>
  <Tab value="overlay">

## `overlay`

A text label positioned at a fixed viewport location. Useful for metric callouts, status text, or diagram annotations.

```text
@present
overlay top-right: "Queue: 500+" position=fixed color=#ef4444
overlay bottom-left: "p99: 2.3s" position=fixed color=#ef4444
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    color: {
      description: "Text color",
      type: "CSS color",
      default: "—",
    },
    position: {
      description: "Must be 'fixed' for overlays",
      type: '"fixed"',
      default: "fixed",
    },
  }}
/>

**Anchor values:** `top-left`, `top-right`, `bottom-left`, `bottom-right`, `top-center`, `bottom-center`

  </Tab>
</Tabs>


---

## /docs/meta-blocks/status

---
title: "@status"
description: Turn your diagram into a live NOC board by mapping nodes to health states — healthy, degraded, or down.
---

import { TypeTable } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/type-table'

The `@status` block maps diagram nodes to health states, turning the diagram into a NOC board. Each node gets a colored border, a badge chip straddling its top edge, and a pulsing animation. A floating legend can show per-node detail labels.

```text
@status
[legend: position={anchor}, padding={px}]
{nodeId}: state={healthy|degraded|down} [, label="{text}"]
```

## States

| state= | Border | Badge | Animation |
|--------|--------|-------|-----------|
| `healthy` | green `#22c55e` | green "HEALTHY" | slow breathe (border, 2.5s) |
| `degraded` | amber `#f59e0b` | amber "DEGRADED" | faster pulse (border, 1.2s) |
| `down` | red `#ef4444` | red "DOWN" | flash strobe (0.8s) |

## Example

```text
flowchart LR
  GW([API Gateway])
  Auth([Auth Service])
  Payment([Payment])
  Orders([Orders])

  GW --> Auth
  GW --> Payment
  GW --> Orders

@status
legend: position=below-center, padding=16
GW: state=healthy, label="p99: 32ms · 99.9% uptime"
Auth: state=degraded, label="p99: 840ms · elevated latency"
Payment: state=down, label="last seen 3m ago · circuit open"
Orders: state=healthy
```

## Parameters

<TypeTable
  type={{
    state: {
      description: "Health state for the node",
      type: '"healthy" | "degraded" | "down"',
      default: "—",
    },
    label: {
      description: "Legend row text — omit to exclude node from legend",
      type: "string",
      default: "—",
    },
  }}
/>

## `legend:` line

Optional. Renders a floating chip row showing nodes that have a `label=` value. Nodes without `label=` are omitted. The legend is not included in SVG/PNG exports.

<TypeTable
  type={{
    position: {
      description: "Legend panel anchor",
      type: '"top-left" | "top-center" | "top-right" | "middle-left" | "middle-right" | "below-left" | "below-center" | "below-right"',
      default: "below-center",
    },
    padding: {
      description: "Offset from panel edge in px",
      type: "number",
      default: "12",
    },
  }}
/>

## Combining with `@animate` and `@style`

Explicit `@animate` or `@style` entries for the same node override the status-derived defaults:

```text
@status
GW: state=healthy

@animate
GW: type=node-pulse, pulseStyle=ripple, color=#22c55e, speed=1.5
```

The `@animate` entry for `GW` overrides the default `border` pulse from `@status`, using `ripple` instead.

Both `@status` and `type=status-dot` can appear in the same diagram. `@status` controls border color, badge chip, and border animation; `status-dot` adds a separate animated indicator dot. See [@animate — status-dot](/docs/meta-blocks/animate) for details.


---

## /docs/meta-blocks/style

---
title: "@style"
description: Set static visual styles on diagram nodes — outline (border) color.
---

import { TypeTable } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/type-table'

The `@style` block sets static visual styles on nodes. Each line targets a node by ID.

```text
@style
{nodeId}: outline={color}
```

## `outline`

Sets the stroke (border) color of the node's shape.

```text
@style
Users: outline=#4fc3f7
API: outline=#f59e0b
DB: outline=#ef4444
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    outline: {
      description: "Stroke color of the node shape. Accepts any CSS color value: named colors, hex, rgb, etc.",
      type: "CSS color",
      default: "—",
    },
  }}
/>

All node shapes are supported: rectangles (`[text]`), stadiums (`([text])`), cylinders (`[(text)]`), diamonds (`{text}`), and others.

<Callout type="info">
`@style` works without `@animate`. You can set an outline color on a node without adding any animation.
</Callout>

## Combining with `@animate`

```text
flowchart LR
  Users([Users])
  API[API Gateway]
  Users --> API

@style
Users: outline=#4fc3f7

@animate
Users-->API: type=line-flow, color=#4fc3f7, speed=2
```


---
